commands.repair_reason: A repair worker can mechanically align the qs version across the workspace override, pnpm lock, and generated root shrinkwrap without changing the PR’s security scope.; Needs attention: The dependency update is security-motivated, but the patch leaves the repo dependency policy on the vulnerable qs version and should be completed before merge.; Review finding: [P2] Align the pnpm policy before adding the qs override... (sha=23f98fdfe7744467583f5d3355b119472a130472)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a root `qs@6.15.2` override and updates the root `npm-shrinkwrap.json` entry for `qs` from `6.14.2` to `6.15.2`.

PR surface: Config +1, Other 0. Total +1 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main pins `qs@6.14.2` in the root shrinkwrap and pnpm policy, and the upstream advisory says `qs` is vulnerable through `6.15.1`. I did not run `npm audit` in this read-only pass, but the PR body includes before/after audit output.
commands.review_followup: A repair worker can mechanically align the `qs` version across the workspace override, pnpm lock, and generated root shrinkwrap without changing the PR’s security scope.

target.title: fix(security): bump qs from 6.14.2 to 6.15.2 (GHSA-q8mj-m7cp-5q26)
target.body:
fix(security): bump qs from 6.14.2 to 6.15.2 to resolve GHSA-q8mj-m7cp-5q26

## Summary
`openclaw@2026.5.22` ships an `npm-shrinkwrap.json` that vendors `qs@6.14.2`, which has a known moderate DoS vulnerability ([GHSA-q8mj-m7cp-5q26](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-q8mj-m7cp-5q26)). Because shrinkwrap is authoritative, consumers cannot override this dependency via `package.json` overrides or `npm audit fix`.

## Changes
- Add `"qs": "6.15.2"` to `package.json` `overrides`
- Update `npm-shrinkwrap.json` to lock `qs@6.15.2` with correct resolved URL and integrity hash

## Real behavior proof

### 1. Problem description
`qs@6.14.2` crashes with `TypeError` on null/undefined entries in `arrayFormat: 'comma'` arrays when `encodeValuesOnly` is set. This is a moderate severity DoS vector.

### 2. Test environment
- OS: Ubuntu 24.04 (GitHub Actions / local)
- Node: v24.14.1
- openclaw commit: `0bb9b421f3`

### 3. Reproduction (before fix)
```bash
mkdir /tmp/test-qs && cd /tmp/test-qs
npm init -y
npm install openclaw@2026.5.22
npm audit --omit=dev
```

**Output:**
```
qs  6.14.2
Moderate: qs.stringify crashes with TypeError on null/undefined entries in arrayFormat: comma arrays when encodeValuesOnly is set
Package: qs
Patched in >=6.15.2
Dependency of openclaw
Path: openclaw > express > qs
More info: https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-q8mj-m7cp-5q26
```

### 4. Fix verification (after fix)
```bash
cd /tmp/doncicx-openclaw
npm audit --omit=dev
```

**Output:**
```
found 0 vulnerabilities
```

### 5. Regression test
- Verified `qs@6.15.2` is installed in `node_modules/qs`
- Verified `npm-shrinkwrap.json` locks `qs@6.15.2` with integrity `sha512-Rzq0KEyX/w/tEybncDgdkZrJgVUsUMk3xjh3t5bv3S1HTAtg+uOYt72+ZfwiQwKdysThkTBdL/rTi6HDmX9Ddw==`
- No functional changes to openclaw code

### 6. Test results
`npm audit` passes with 0 vulnerabilities after the bump.

## Impact
- Affected: all consumers installing `openclaw` via npm
- Severity: Moderate (DoS)
- Backwards compatibility: yes — `qs@6.15.2` is a patch release in the same semver line

Refs #86457
target.author: xuzehan-icenter
